beats.title: THE CAMPAIGN
beats.subtitle: Standing up a system the right way · the RMF
say: You have met the twenty guardians. But knowing them is not the same as deploying them.
You just watched Aegis Hospital secured end to end. Now we slow down and learn each step — and Nova, you're leading it.
From apprentice to running the campaign. Let's begin.

beats.title: THE RISK MANAGEMENT FRAMEWORK
beats.subtitle: NIST SP 800-37, Revision 2
say: The plan of campaign has a name: the Risk Management Framework, from 800-37.
The process that tells you what to do, and in what order.

beats.plain: A repeatable, step-by-step process for managing a system's security risk — from understanding the system, to choosing and proving controls, to approving operation and watching over time.
say: What makes the RMF a 'framework' and not just a checklist?
It's the living process that ties the catalog, the baselines, and the assessments into one repeatable lifecycle.

beats.title: Seven steps, one lifecycle
say: Seven steps. Prepare, categorize, select, implement, assess, authorize, and monitor.
And it's a cycle, monitoring never stops.
Prepare was added in Revision 2, getting the organization and system ready first.

beats.title: What each step does
bullets: Prepare — set context, roles, and risk strategy
Select — choose a control baseline (800-53B) and tailor it
Implement — put the controls (800-53) to work
say: Each step has a job, and most are guided by a supporting NIST publication.

beats.title: ...and the last two
bullets: Authorize — a senior official accepts the risk (the ATO)
Monitor — ongoing awareness throughout the system lifecycle (800-137)
Then the cycle begins again as the system changes
say: Authorize, then monitor. A system is never simply 'done.'
Good. Because I never stop, either.

beats.title: Your campaign bookshelf
bullets: SP 800-53A — how to assess; SP 800-37 — the process
say: These books travel together. Each answers one question in the campaign.
So Act One taught the controls. Act Two is how we actually use them.

beats.q: How many steps are in the RMF (SP 800-37 Rev 2)?
say: Quick check. How many steps in the framework? Pause, and answer.

beats.q: Which RMF step was newly added in Revision 2?
say: And which step did Revision 2 add at the front?

beats.title: Episode 08 — remember this
bullets: The RMF (800-37r2) has 7 steps: Prepare, Categorize, Select, Implement, Assess, Authorize, Monitor
Each step has a companion NIST document
It's a continuous lifecycle, not a one-time checklist
say: The map is set. First stop, knowing what we're protecting.
